articles.title: Medtronic adds Corsano wearable to its US portfolio
articles.summary: Partnership reflects a bigger shift: medical wearables are becoming part of healthcare’s infrastructure, not just another device on the wrist. Modern hospitals have monitors, alarms, dashboards and devices constantly generating new readings, so no shortage of information. Yet one of healthcare’s biggest challenges isn’t collecting more data, but making sense of it. Too many alerts […] The post Medtronic adds Corsano wearable to its US portfolio appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: BPGbio moves closer to Phase 3 in rare mitochondrial disease
articles.summary: New findings suggest investigational therapy may overcome a longstanding challenge in treating primary CoQ10 deficiency. In drug development, sometimes, the biggest story is what regulators are willing to do with the evidence. AI-powered, clinical-stage biopharma company BPGbio has revealed new findings supporting BPM31510, its investigational therapy for primary CoQ10 deficiency (PCQD), while revealing something equally […] The post BPGbio moves closer to Phase 3 in rare mitochondrial disease appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Evipedia expands evidence reviews to cover peptides
articles.summary: New peptide reviews aim to bring structure and transparency to one of longevity’s fastest-moving – and most hotly debated – areas. Peptides have become one of the most closely watched areas in longevity medicine, attracting growing interest from researchers, clinicians and consumers alike. Yet despite their popularity, the evidence supporting many peptide interventions remains uneven […] The post Evipedia expands evidence reviews to cover peptides appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Intermittent Fasting Increases Lifespan in Male Mice
articles.summary: Restricting food access to an 8-hour window increased median lifespan in male mice by 12%. However, that might be due to voluntary caloric restriction induced […]

articles.title: Fountain Life makes longevity accessible with $595 membership
articles.summary: Company introduces BASE, its lowest-priced membership yet, raising the question: is preventive healthcare finally becoming more mainstream? A new chapter for consumer longevity? For a company synonymous with premium longevity programs, a $595 annual membership is a bold statement. Fountain Life has built its reputation around comprehensive preventive health programs that use advanced diagnostics to […] The post Fountain Life makes longevity accessible with $595 membership appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: NeuroSense bolsters case for multi-target Alzheimer’s strategy
articles.summary: Biomarker results suggest PrimeC may be acting on shared neurodegeneration mechanisms, adding weight to growing shift in brain aging research. What if ALS, Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s aren’t entirely separate diseases after all? Isn’t it that, for many years, we have treated neurodegenerative disorders as distinct conditions, each with its own causes, symptoms and treatment strategies? […] The post NeuroSense bolsters case for multi-target Alzheimer’s strategy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: UCL, Eisai extend partnership to find new treatments for Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s
articles.summary: UCL and Eisai have signed a new agreement extending their drug discovery and development collaboration for five years, through 2030, to accelerate therapies for neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. The alliance, first launched in 2012 and led by the UCL Translational Research Office and the Faculty of Brain Sciences, will […] The post UCL, Eisai extend partnership to find new treatments for Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Elixirgen builds rare disease pipeline around telomeres and DMD
articles.summary: Elixirgen Therapeutics has expanded its rare disease pipeline with a licensing option for EXG-7001 and clinical progress for EXG-34217. On 26 June, Elixirgen entered an option agreement with Nippon Shinyaku under which Elixirgen will lead development of EXG-7001, a locally administered full-length dystrophin mRNA therapeutic in preclinical development for Duchenne muscular dystrophy; Nippon Shinyaku may […] The post Elixirgen builds rare disease pipeline around telomeres and DMD appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: NorthStrive advances muscle preservation strategy with successful AI lab results
articles.summary: NorthStrive Biosciences, a subsidiary of PMGC Holdings Inc., announced positive Phase III results from its AI-driven drug discovery program with Yuva Biosciences. According to the company, Phase III biologically validated a focused set of small-molecule candidates selected by Yuva’s MitoNova AI platform using an in vitro assay in primary human skeletal muscle cells that measured […] The post NorthStrive advances muscle preservation strategy with successful AI lab results appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Trained AI outperforms biologists at spotting salmon lice
articles.summary: Researchers have taken over 120,000 images of salmon lice larvae in seawater and used them to train AI models. The models were much faster and more accurate than experienced biologists at identifying the parasites that feed on the skin and blood of salmonids.

articles.title: Small-molecule switches put therapeutic CRISPR editing under on-demand control in living tissues
articles.summary: In a study published in Science Translational Medicine, a team of researchers led by Dr. Wang Yu from the Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology of the Chinese Academy of Sciences developed PRINCE and Little Prince, dual small-molecule-controlled genome editing systems that allow CRISPR activity to be switched on by drug inducers and kept largely silent in their absence.

articles.title: Giant wheat starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for diet, manufacturing
articles.summary: Scientists have grown wheat containing supersized starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for our daily diets and a raft of industrial applications.

articles.title: How a new fungal genome-editing tool could open fresh paths to cancer treatments
articles.summary: Researchers have spent decades—and billions of dollars—sequencing animal and crop genomes, but fungi have historically been the forgotten middle child of genomics, only noticed when they're ruining bread or colonizing toes.

articles.title: Author Correction: Improved RNA base editing with guide RNAs mimicking highly edited endogenous ADAR substrates
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 03 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03246-6 Author Correction: Improved RNA base editing with guide RNAs mimicking highly edited endogenous ADAR substrates

articles.title: How to stop a mouse plague
articles.summary: The scenes are biblical. Tens of thousands of rodents scattering across canola fields, behind sheds, into machinery. River fish with bellies full of mice. Carcasses littering the street, the sidewalk, outside your home. In supermarkets, the inescapable stench of dead and dying mice. These are the undeniable signs of a mouse plague.

articles.title: A WRAP for biology's greasiest problem
articles.summary: Embedded in the boundary between the inside and outside of each cell are membrane proteins. They act as first responders by sensing signals, regulating which molecules enter and leave the cell, and enabling cells to quickly adapt to changes in their environment.

articles.title: Rethinking the governance of human embryo research: Comparing Japan's guidelines with international standards
articles.summary: Human embryo models can help researchers study early human development and infertility without relying solely on human embryos. As the technology advances, these models are becoming more complex and can be maintained in culture for longer periods. These developments have raised new questions about how such research should be regulated.

articles.title: AI could bring satellite crop monitoring to the world's most vulnerable farms
articles.summary: Small farms grow much of the world's food, but from space they are nearly invisible. Their fields are tiny and ill-defined, and the satellite tools built to track crops were designed for the large, uniform fields of industrial agriculture, not the sub-hectare plots that feed many of the world's poorest people.

articles.title: Huge, specially designed heat pump saves a Norwegian agricultural cooperative millions
articles.summary: There are some magical limits to how much energy we can get out of a heat pump. This story is about pushing the technical limits. It is about getting more energy out than you put in. And it's about how SINTEF—one of Europe's largest applied research organizations—the renewable energy company Aneo, and the Norwegian agricultural cooperative Felleskjøpet together managed to shift the recovery of process heat from theoretical calculations in researchers' notebooks to becoming an industrial electricity-saving project on a large scale.

articles.title: Bollinger Bands creator eyes Bitcoin bear-market end, 'W'-shaped reversal
articles.summary: Bitcoin price strength could "break" the entire downtrend if the reversal pattern completed, John Bollinger suggested in light of the BTC rebound.

articles.title: Tim Draper denies moving Bitcoin, reiterates $250,000 BTC prediction

articles.title: XRP climbs 8% as record holder losses signal better risk-reward for buyers
articles.summary: The token's 30-day and 365-day MVRV, a measure of how far holders are underwater, sit near -45% and -47%, lows Santiment says XRP has never reached before. Some traders read stretched losses as a contrarian signal.

articles.title: Bitcoin’s next parabolic run may need $1 trillion in fresh capital
articles.summary: This cycle, about $697 billion in new money has generated a roughly 689% gain, compared with earlier cycles where far less capital drove returns of upto 50,000%.

articles.title: Bitcoin profit and loss ratio falls to 43-month low

articles.title: US law enforcement group drops opposition to CLARITY Act: Report
articles.summary: The Major County Sheriffs of America said it still wants the CLARITY Act amended to give local law enforcement more resources to investigate illicit finance cases.

articles.title: Bitcoin, Ether extend relief rallies as extreme fear meets renewed ETF buying
articles.summary: Bitcoin and Ether bounced off multi-year lows as dip buyers finally stepped in, and the spot BTC ETFs saw a $221 million inflow on July 2.
